children.name: Pedionomus
children.description: A monotypic genus representing one of Australia's most evolutionarily distinctive bird lineages. The Plains-wanderer was long considered a buttonquail (Turnicidae) but DNA studies reveal it to be the sole surviving member of an ancient lineage that diverged before the radiation of modern shorebirds. It exhibits reversed sexual dimorphism and a polyandrous breeding system.

children.name: Pedionomus torquatus
children.description: A critically endangered ground bird endemic to the native grasslands of south-eastern Australia. The female is larger and more brightly coloured than the male, with a distinctive black-and-white collar. It has suffered catastrophic declines due to habitat destruction and predation by introduced foxes and cats, with fewer than 1,000 individuals estimated in the wild.
